content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DECISION title=Start memory layer with Postgres stack, not Graph DB summary=In 01. Architecture, the recommended default for the memory layer is a Postgres-based stack (JSONB, pgvector, full-text indexes, lineage tables) because current structures already provide graph-like semantics without requiring a Graph DB first. scope=01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1>,entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6> keywords=Postgres,JSONB,pgvector,full-text indexes,lineage tables,Graph DB tags=storage_decision,memory_layer,architecture evidence=Для memory layer обычно хватает: - Postgres - JSONB - pgvector - full-text indexes - lineage tables
[1] type=DIRECTION title=Adoption criterion for Graph DB in v3 memory architecture summary=Graph DB should be introduced only when traversal/path queries become part of the product itself, rather than an internal implementation convenience. scope=v3 memory architecture decision criteria for storage backend in the current project entities=entity:<7>,entity:<6>,entity:<8> keywords=Graph DB,traversal queries,path queries,decision criterion tags=backend_selection,scaling_criteria,architecture_direction evidence=Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DECISION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory architecture uses four axes to prevent transcript-dump or brittle-fact failure modes summary=In v3, memory is structured along four axes—Representation (Source/Claim/Note/Profile/Task/Episode), Lifecycle (write→reconcile→consolidate→repair→retain/archive/delete), Retrieval policy (claim-first/note-first/mixed/evidence-first with bounded budgets), and Scope and lineage (scope_json, scope_text, source refs, origin note, memory runs). The document states these axes are necessary to avoid deg...[truncated 55 chars] scope=v3 memory architecture for the current project keywords=v3,four axes,representation lifecycle retrieval scope lineage,bounded budgets tags=architecture,v3,memory_model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim vs Note write policy in 01. Architecture summary=Section 7 defines a decision rule: write Claim for explicit, normalizable, precise-answer-ready information comparable by predicate policy; write Note for already important retrieval context that is too contextual or too early for hard facts; and write both when a source contains explicit facts/tasks plus rationale or direction. scope=01. Architecture section 7 ('Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`) in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Claim vs Note,write policy,predicate policy,rationale tags=memory_architecture,policy,claim,note useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture in 01. Architecture summary=Section 5 defines five storage tiers with distinct roles: Tier A for compact in-prompt context, Tier B for typed long-term records, Tier C for semantic notes, Tier D for immutable raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes and filters. scope=01. Architecture section '5. Storage tiers' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Storage tiers,Tier A,Tier B,Tier C,Tier D,Tier E tags=architecture,memory_layers,doc_digest useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Read path uses need-gating, retrieval modes, and explicit v3 budget caps summary=Section 12 specifies that memory retrieval should be triggered only for qualifying question types, then use mode selection (claim-first, note-first, mixed, evidence-first/fallback) and explicit per-mode caps before composing answers with verification/conflict/scope safeguards. scope=01. Architecture / 12. Read path in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=read path,retrieval mode,budget caps,answer composer tags=architecture,retrieval_policy,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Dual write model: semantic and maintenance paths in 01. Architecture summary=The architecture defines two semantic write paths (Direct structured path for safely normalizable items, and Note path for important but not-yet-hard-fact context) plus two maintenance paths (Consolidation to promote mature notes and Repair to fix memory degradation). scope=01. Architecture section '3. Основная модель записи' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Direct structured path,Note path,Consolidation,Repair,semantic write paths tags=memory_architecture,write_model,maintenance_paths useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DECISION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory repair is a separate background workflow from consolidation summary=Section 14 establishes `Repair` as a distinct background workflow focused on memory health issues: duplicate notes/claims, contradictory active items, profile drift, stale task states, bad entity merges, note spam, and retrieval budget leakage. It is explicitly separated from consolidation: consolidation enriches memory, while repair restores/maintains health. scope=01. Architecture / 14. Memory repair in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory repair,consolidation vs repair,background workflow tags=memory_maintenance,repair_workflow,quality_control useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DECISION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Truth state and Retrieval state must remain separate summary=Section 8 establishes that truth status and retrieval hotness are independent dimensions: truth state tracks knowledge validity (with different allowed states for claims vs notes), while retrieval state tracks indexing/hot-path placement (active/warm/cold/archived/deleted). Items can be truth-resolved or superseded yet remain warm for rationale or historical evidence retrieval. scope=01. Architecture section 8 ('Truth state и retrieval state') in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Truth state,retrieval state,active,superseded,warm tags=state_model,retrieval,lifecycle,memory_architecture useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DECISION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 forgetting is policy-driven with defined actions and triggers summary=In 01. Architecture, forgetting in v3 is defined as an explicit policy (not passive decay), with minimum actions (downgrade retrieval state, archive, soft delete, hard delete by request) and minimum triggers (namespace policy, age, low use_count, legal/privacy request, explicit user forget/delete command). scope=01. Architecture / 15. Retention / forgetting in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=v3 forgetting policy,retention triggers,hard delete by request tags=memory_policy,retention,forgetting useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<8> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Optional fast/slow execution split with typed handoff objects summary=Section 16 defines an optional two-path execution model: fast path for user-facing low-latency cheap retrieval/draft with escalation gate, and slow path for stronger processing (conflict resolution, verification, note consolidation, memory repair). It recommends passing typed handoff objects between paths instead of free text. scope=01. Architecture / 16. Optional fast/slow execution path in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=fast path,slow path,typed handoff objects tags=execution_model,latency,handoff_contract useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<9> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Write path in 01. Architecture is an 8-step transactional pipeline summary=Section 11 defines a fixed write workflow: persist source, route, retrieve candidate memories before extraction, canonicalize entities, extract via specialized prompts, reconcile with existing memory, apply all changes in one transaction, and schedule maintenance jobs. scope=01. Architecture / 11. Write path in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=write path,transaction,reconciliation,maintenance tags=architecture,memory_pipeline,write_flow useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<10> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note maturity lifecycle prevents Note summary-store degradation summary=Section 13 defines a maturity lifecycle for `Note` (`fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`) to prevent notes from degrading into a summary-store. Promotion signals include retrieval usage, multi-source confirmation, conflict-free survival, time without supersede, and recurring rationale value; demotion/closure signals include explicit supersede, stale scope, non-usage, and weak eviden...[truncated 11 chars] scope=01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Note maturity,fresh stabilizing mature consolidated,summary-store prevention tags=memory_policy,note_lifecycle,retrieval_quality useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<11> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory layer goals for persistent, explainable, and maintainable agent memory summary=The architecture defines a memory layer that must persist across sessions, store facts/preferences/tasks/rationale/evolving plans, support knowledge updates, preserve provenance, avoid making every turn permanent, and support aging, archiving, and repair. scope=01. Architecture section '1. Цель системы' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=memory layer goals,provenance,selective permanence,archive and repair tags=architecture,memory_design,policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<12> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 structured scope requires both scope_text and scope_json with mandatory envelope fields summary=Section 6 sets a design direction that v3 stores both human-readable `scope_text` and machine-readable `scope_json`; the minimum `scope_json` includes `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience` to improve reconciliation predictability and reduce retrieval overgeneralization/conflicts. scope=01. Architecture section '6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Structured scope,scope_text,scope_json,scope_kind,project_id,reconciliation,retrieval tags=v3,scope,design_direction useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<13> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory update flow must include retrieval/reconciliation and policy actions, not append-only writes summary=The architecture sets four mandatory write-time tasks: decide whether to write memory, retrieve similar/conflicting items, convert input to typed candidates, and apply policy actions (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplicates, stale preferences, conflicts, task explosion, and summary spam. scope=01. Architecture section 9 (update should not be append-only) keywords=append-only update,retrieval-before-update,reconciliation,insert update supersede retract noop tags=memory_write_policy,architecture useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[],"patch":null,"reason":"New distinct architecture decision from section 17: default memory-layer backend should start with Postgres+JSONB+pgvector+FTS+lineage instead of Graph DB.","target_note_id":null},{"action":"insert","candidate_index":1,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.72,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"New reusable criterion for when to adopt Graph DB (only when traversal/path queries are product requirements), not present as a standalone active note.","target_note_id":null}]}
